name: Hawaiian Upsidedown Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup C & H Golden Brown Sugar (firmly packed)
1 cup Juice-pack crushed pineapple (drained measure), reserve juice
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a 10-inch oven-proof skillet.
2. Sprinkle brown sugar evenly over the melted butter; then layer with pineapple, coconut, and chopped cherries.
3. In a mixing bowl, beat eggs until light and thick (about 3 to 5 minutes).
4. Gradually beat in granulated sugar.
5. Add 1/2 cup reserved pineapple juice and vanilla.
6. Stir in dry ingredients until smooth.
7. Pour the batter over the topping in the skillet.
8. Bake at 350°F for 40-45 minutes, or until set.
9. Cool for 5 minutes.
10. Invert onto a serving plate. Makes one 10-inch cake (serves 8).
Reprinted with permission from Just Cakes
From the C and H Sugar Kitchen
